task_completion.description: Open the NuGet Package Manager in Visual Studio.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Manage NuGet Packages' tab is open and visible in the main document area of Visual Studio.
The 'Browse' tab is selected in the NuGet Package Manager and 'Newtonsoft.Json' is typed into the search bar.

task_completion.description: Select the correct package from the search results.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Newtonsoft.Json' package is clicked/highlighted in the list of search results.
The 'Install' button is clicked on the right-hand detail pane, or a 'Preview Changes' modal dialog is visible.

task_completion.description: Confirm the package is successfully added to the project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The package shows a green checkmark, appears under the 'Installed' tab, or 'Newtonsoft.Json' is visible under 'Dependencies' -> 'Packages' in the Solution Explorer.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Visual Studio 2022 application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the genuine Visual Studio 2022 interface, not a text editor or mock-up.

integrity.description: The agent did not use an external terminal (like cmd or PowerShell) to bypass the Visual Studio interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: No external terminal windows are used to run 'dotnet add package' commands.

integrity.description: The package was added to the correct InventoryManager project.
integrity.visual_evidence: The NuGet Package Manager shows 'InventoryManager' as the target project, or the Solution Explorer confirms the package is under the InventoryManager project's dependencies.
